item.title: Sanday: There will always be rumours around FRU
item.description: Sanday: There will always be rumours around FRU

item.title: Kumar says Junior Bula Boys continue to improve ahead of OFC U-16 semi-final
item.description: Head coach of the Junior Bula Boys, Sunil Kumar, says the team is growing in confidence as they prepare for their OFC U-16 Men's Championship semi-final against Solomon Islands this Wednesday. Fiji heads into the knockout clash after finishing top of Group A with three wins from three matches.
item.content:encoded:
Head coach of the Junior Bula Boys, Sunil Kumar, says the team is growing in confidence as they prepare for their OFC U-16 Men's Championship semi-final against Solomon Islands this Wednesday.

Fiji heads into the knockout clash after finishing top of Group A with three wins from three matches.

The Junior Bula Boys beat New Caledonia 2–1, thrashed Samoa 5–0 and defeated PNG 3–0 in their final group match to top the pool.

Fiji will play Solomon Islands in the semi-final at the PNG Football Stadium on Wednesday at 6pm, while New Zealand will face PNG in the other semi-final at 9.30pm.

The final will be played at 9.30pm this Saturday.

The winner and runner-up will qualify for the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2027.

item.title: Proud Nasinu parent calls on old scholars to support Under-19 team ahead of finals
item.description: The unwavering support of parents has played a vital role in Nasinu Secondary School's Under-19 rugby team's success after defeating Assemblies of God Secondary School 13-7 at the Grammar Grounds, with one proud mother calling on old scholars to rally behind the team as they prepare for the finals. Speaking to fijivillage Sports after Nasinu's victory, proud parent Akosita Vakatawa says she is ove
item.content:encoded:
The unwavering support of parents has played a vital role in Nasinu Secondary School's Under-19 rugby team's success after defeating Assemblies of God Secondary School 13-7 at the Grammar Grounds, with one proud mother calling on old scholars to rally behind the team as they prepare for the finals.

Speaking to fijivillage Sports after Nasinu's victory, proud parent Akosita Vakatawa says she is overwhelmed with joy and proud of what the Under-19 team has achieved so far.

The finals will be on Saturday at Grammar grounds.

item.title: Teen goalkeeper Paulini ready for Asian Women's Handball Championship
item.description: At just 16 years old, Namosi Secondary School student Paulini Nagata is making her mark in handball, with the young goalkeeper relying on teamwork and communication as they prepare for the Asian Women's Handball Championship . The Year 11 student from Navunibau in Namosi says handball is similar to netball and football, making it an enjoyable and exciting sport to play.
item.content:encoded:
At just 16 years old, Namosi Secondary School student Paulini Nagata is making her mark in handball, with the young goalkeeper relying on teamwork and communication as they prepare for the Asian Women's Handball Championship .

The Year 11 student from Navunibau in Namosi says handball is similar to netball and football, making it an enjoyable and exciting sport to play.

Nagata says handball is mainly played by women and has helped her develop new skills and techniques.

Playing as a goalkeeper, she says communication is one of her biggest responsibilities on the court.

Nagata says she must always be prepared and work closely with her six teammates by constantly communicating with them, organising the defence and ensuring everyone is in the right position during a match.

She believes good teamwork and communication are essential for success and hopes more young people, especially girls, will take up handball and enjoy the opportunities the sport has to offer.

The 2026 Asian Women's Handball Championship will kick off tomorrow at the Kowloon Park Sports Centre in Hong Kong, with Group A matches scheduled over the opening five days of competition.

item.title: Legge wants packed stadium for Drua Women’s title decider against Waratahs on Saturday
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women head coach Mike Legge has called on fans to turn out in force for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s final, saying his players deserve the same level of support as the men's team. Speaking to fijivillage News after his side's commanding 31-5 victory over the Queensland Reds at Churchill Park in Lautoka last Saturday, Legge says the Drua Women have earned the backing of the e
item.content:encoded:
McDonald's Fiji Drua Women head coach Mike Legge has called on fans to turn out in force for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s final, saying his players deserve the same level of support as the men's team.

Speaking to fijivillage News after his side's commanding 31-5 victory over the Queensland Reds at Churchill Park in Lautoka last Saturday, Legge says the Drua Women have earned the backing of the entire fan base.

Insert: Legge - Get behind the girls July 20

Legge stresses his side will not alter its approach despite the magnitude of the occasion.

The Drua Women will now face the NSW Waratahs in the Super Rugby Women’s final at 1pm on Saturday at 4R Stadium in Ba.

item.title: Mbappé makes history with second World Cup Golden Boot
item.description: France captain Kylian Mbappé has become the first player to win the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot twice after finishing as the top scorer at the 2026 tournament. The 27-year-old scored 10 goals in eight matches to edge Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who finished with eight goals.
item.content:encoded:
France captain Kylian Mbappé has become the first player to win the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot twice after finishing as the top scorer at the 2026 tournament.

The 27-year-old scored 10 goals in eight matches to edge Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who finished with eight goals.

Mbappé also won the Golden Boot at the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar after scoring eight goals, making him the first player to claim the award at back-to-back World Cups.

His 10 goals also made him the first player to score double figures in a single World Cup since Germany's Gerd Müller scored 10 goals in 1970.

Mbappé has also become the all-time leading goalscorer in men's FIFA World Cup history with 22 goals, one more than Messi.

Despite scoring twice in France's 6-4 loss to England in the third-place playoff, Mbappé said he would have preferred reaching the final instead of winning the Golden Boot.

He says while the award would add to his legacy after retirement, his focus had been on helping France reach the World Cup final.

Meanwhile, newly crowned world champions Spain also swept the major individual honours.

Goalkeeper Unai Simón won the Golden Glove award after an outstanding tournament in which he produced a series of crucial saves, including in the knockout stages and the World Cup final to help Spain keep a clean sheet against Argentina.

item.title: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 to win FIFA World Cup after 16 years
item.description: Spain have won the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 16 years, defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after a dramatic final that went into extra time in New Jersey. The two teams were locked at 0-0 after 90 minutes, forcing the match into extra time.
item.content:encoded:
Spain have won the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 16 years, defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after a dramatic final that went into extra time in New Jersey.

The two teams were locked at 0-0 after 90 minutes, forcing the match into extra time.

Argentina were later reduced to 10 men after Enzo Fernández was sent off.

Spain eventually found the breakthrough through Ferran Torres in the 106th minute of extra time to secure their second FIFA World Cup title and their first in 16 years.

Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón was awarded the Golden Glove, while Rodrigo Hernández Cascante was named Player of the Tournament after another outstanding campaign in midfield.

France captain Kylian Mbappé won the Golden Boot award after finishing as the tournament's top scorer.

The win also ended Argentina's reign as world champions after they claimed the title in Qatar in 2022.

More than 80,000 fans packed New York New Jersey Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina.

The winners will also take home about FJ$112 million in prize money with t he runners-up will receive about FJ$75 million.

Meanwhile, global music stars Madonna, Shakira and Justin Bieber entertained fans during the halftime show.

Hollywood actor Tom Cruise made a special appearance and delivered a speech before kickoff.

item.title: Grammar chases Southern Zone Deans glory
item.description: The Southern Zone Vodafone Deans Finals promise an exciting day of rugby this Saturday, with tournament favourites Suva Grammar School taking on Nasinu Secondary School in the Under-18 final. In the Under-17 division,Grammar will face Gospel High School in another highly anticipated clash.
item.content:encoded:
The Southern Zone Vodafone Deans Finals promise an exciting day of rugby this Saturday, with tournament favourites Suva Grammar School taking on Nasinu Secondary School in the Under-18 final.

In the Under-17 division,Grammar will face Gospel High School in another highly anticipated clash.

By reaching the final, Gospel High has also secured a place in the National Deans quarter-finals.

Grammar's strong campaign continues across multiple grades.

In the Under-16 final, they will once again meet Nasinu, while the Under-15 final will feature the traditional rivalry between Grammar and Marist Brothers High School.

The Weet-Bix Raluve competition will also deliver exciting contests.

The Under-16 final will see Grammar face Nasinu, while the premier Under-18 Raluve final will feature Lomary Secondary School against Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School.

Grammar will face Nasinu in the U:19 final.

All Southern Zone Vodafone Deans and Weet-Bix Raluve finals will be played at the Suva Grammar School Grounds this Saturday, with fans expected to turn out in large numbers for a day of high-quality school rugby.

item.title: Mbappe edges closer to second Golden Boot after France defeat
item.description: Kylian Mbappe is on the verge of winning a second FIFA World Cup Golden Boot after scoring twice in France's thrilling 6-4 defeat

item.title: 13 year old aspiring referee's dream comes true at Eden Park
item.description: A trip to watch her first All Blacks Test at Eden Park turned into a dream come true for 13-year-old aspiring Fijian referee Vaibh

item.title: England beat France 6-4 in thrilling World Cup third-place play-off
item.description: England survived a fierce second-half comeback from France to claim third place at the FIFA World Cup with a thrilling 6-4 victory
